name: pgvector_phase3_receipts_bugs
outcome_goal: Receipt search and bug tracker use vector similarity for semantic matching; bug filing surfaces near-duplicates

jobs.label: p3_receipt_embed_on_write
jobs.prompt:
Add embedding generation to receipt enrichment so new receipts get vector embeddings on insert.

Read Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/receipt_enrichment.py first.

The ReceiptSearchIndex class has a method that inserts enriched receipts into the receipt_search table. Find that method and add embedding generation.

Changes:
1. Accept an optional EmbeddingService in ReceiptSearchIndex.__init__
2. When inserting a receipt, build embed text from the searchable content (the same text that goes into the tsvector — typically job_label + agent + status + failure_analysis concatenated)
3. If embedder is available, call embedder.embed_one(text) and include the vector in the INSERT:
   Include: embedding = $N::vector
   Use EmbeddingService.vector_literal(vec) to format
4. If embedder is None, skip (leave embedding NULL)

Import: from runtime.embedding_service import EmbeddingService

Keep changes minimal — only add to the insert/upsert path.

jobs.label: p3_receipt_semantic_search
jobs.prompt:
Add semantic vector search to the receipt search tool, blending with existing FTS.

Read the receipt search implementation:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/receipt_enrichment.py (the ReceiptSearchIndex.search method)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/mcp/tools/evidence.py (the dag_receipt_search MCP tool handler)

Changes to ReceiptSearchIndex.search():
1. Accept optional embedder and conn parameters (or store them from __init__)
2. If embedder is available:
   a. Embed the query: vec = embedder.embed_one(query)
   b. Run vector similarity search:
      SELECT receipt_id, 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS cosine_sim
      FROM receipt_search
      WHERE embedding IS NOT NULL AND 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) >= 0.3
      ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector LIMIT {limit * 2}
   c. Apply optional status/agent filters to the vector query too
3. Merge FTS results and vector results using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF with k=60):
   - Score = sum(1/(k + rank_in_source)) across sources where result appears
   - This is the same fusion strategy used in module_indexer.py search method
4. Return merged results sorted by fused score
5. If embedder is None, fall back to existing FTS-only behavior (backward compatible)

Update the MCP tool handler to pass the embedder through from subsystems.

Use the existing EmbeddingService from subsystems.get_embedding_service().

jobs.label: p3_bug_embed_and_dedup
jobs.prompt:
Add vector embeddings and near-duplicate detection to the bug tracker.

Read Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/bug_tracker.py first.

Changes:

1. BugTracker.__init__: accept optional EmbeddingService

2. On bug filing (the method that INSERTs into bugs table):
   a. Build embed text: title + ' ' + description
   b. Embed: vec = embedder.embed_one(text)
   c. Before inserting, search for near-duplicates:
      SELECT bug_id, title, status, severity,
             1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS similarity
      FROM bugs
      WHERE embedding IS NOT NULL
        AND 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) >= 0.85
      ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
      LIMIT 5
   d. If near-duplicates found, include them in the return value as a 'similar_bugs' field
   e. Still insert the new bug (don't block filing), but surface the matches so the caller can decide
   f. Include the embedding in the INSERT

3. On bug search:
   a. If embedder available, run vector search alongside FTS
   b. Merge with RRF (k=60) like receipt search
   c. Fall back to FTS-only if no embedder

4. Update the MCP tool handler in Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/mcp/tools/bugs.py:
   a. Pass embedder from subsystems
   b. When filing a bug, if similar_bugs returned, include them in the tool response so the user sees potential duplicates

Import: from runtime.embedding_service import EmbeddingService
Format vectors with: EmbeddingService.vector_literal(vec)
